meta.title: Malaysia Tax Calculator 2026
meta.description: Free Malaysia personal income tax calculator. Calculate your tax for the latest year of assessment with all LHDN reliefs and deductions.

calculator.title: Personal Income Tax Calculator
calculator.subtitle: Calculate your Malaysia personal income tax

results.title: Tax Calculation Results
results.disclaimer: This calculation is for reference only and does not constitute tax advice. Please consult LHDN or a licensed tax professional for official assessment.

explanation.title: Understanding Your Tax
explanation.taxableDesc: In Malaysia, tax residents are taxed on income derived from Malaysia. This includes employment income (salary, wages, bonus), business income, rental income, interest, dividends, and other sources.
explanation.howItWorksDesc: Malaysia uses a progressive tax system. Your chargeable income (gross income minus reliefs) is taxed at increasing rates across different bands. Only the portion within each band is taxed at that rate.
explanation.reliefsDesc: Tax reliefs reduce your chargeable income. Every resident gets RM9,000 personal relief automatically. Additional reliefs are available for education, medical expenses, lifestyle purchases, insurance, and family dependents.
explanation.pcbDesc: PCB (Potongan Cukai Bulanan / Monthly Tax Deduction) is the amount your employer deducts from your salary each month as advance tax payment. At year end, your actual tax is compared against total PCB paid — you either get a refund or pay the difference.

corporate.title: Corporate Income Tax Calculator
corporate.subtitle: Calculate Malaysia corporate income tax
corporate.smeNote: SME eligibility: Paid-up capital ≤ RM2.5M AND revenue < RM50M, plus conditions below
corporate.subsidiaryQuestion: Related to a company with paid-up capital > RM2.5M (parent/subsidiary)?
corporate.foreignOwnershipQuestion: More than 20% of shares held by foreign companies or non-citizens?
corporate.disclaimer: This calculation is for reference only. Please consult a licensed tax professional for official corporate tax assessment.

batchPcb.title: Batch Employee PCB Calculator
batchPcb.subtitle: Calculate monthly PCB deductions for all employees at once
batchPcb.noEmployees: Add employees above to calculate batch PCB

employer.title: Employer Statutory Contributions
employer.subtitle: Calculate EPF, SOCSO & EIS employer obligations for your employees
employer.disclaimer: Rates based on 2025 schedule. Actual amounts may vary based on specific wage brackets. Consult KWSP/PERKESO for exact figures.
employer.epfNote: EPF: 13% (≤RM5k) or 12% (>RM5k), ceiling RM20k. Age 60-65: 4%. Above 65: exempt.
employer.socsoNote: SOCSO: Employer 1.75%, Employee 0.5%, ceiling RM6k. Below 60, Malaysian/PR only.
employer.eisNote: EIS: 0.2% each, ceiling RM6k. Below 60, Malaysian/PR only.

sst.title: SST Calculator
sst.subtitle: Calculate Sales & Service Tax obligations
sst.expansionNote: Rates per the 1 July 2025 service tax expansion. Rental, construction, financial, healthcare and education services are newly taxable.
sst.noThreshold: No threshold (register from first taxable sale)
sst.registrationNotRequired: Below registration threshold — SST not applicable
sst.disclaimer: This is a simplified estimate. Actual SST obligations depend on the specific goods/services supplied. Consult RMCD (Royal Malaysian Customs Department) for official guidance.
sst.sstDesc: SST (Sales & Service Tax) replaced GST in Malaysia from September 2018. Sales Tax (5% or 10%) applies to taxable goods manufactured or imported. Service Tax is 8% for most services and 6% for F&B, telecom, parking and logistics. From 1 July 2025 the scope expanded to rental/leasing, construction, financial services, private healthcare and private education, each with its own registration threshold.
sst.salesVsServiceDesc: Sales Tax is imposed on manufacturers of taxable goods and on importers. Service Tax is charged on prescribed services. Since 1 July 2025: rental/leasing 8% (RM1M threshold), construction 6% (RM1.5M), financial services 8% (RM1M), private healthcare for non-citizens 6% (RM1.5M), and private education 6% (no threshold). Residential rental and residential construction remain non-taxable.

property.title: Property Tax Tools
property.subtitle: RPGT when you sell, stamp duty when you buy — for Malaysian property

rpgt.inputSubtitle: Tax on the profit when you dispose of property. Enter the figures from your Sale & Purchase Agreements.
rpgt.expensesHint: Legal fees, agent commission, renovation, advertising — deductible
rpgt.holdingHint: Years from the purchase S&P date to the disposal date
rpgt.onceInLifetime: Claim once-in-a-lifetime exemption (private residence, citizens/PR only — fully exempts the gain)
rpgt.noTax: No RPGT payable for this holding period.
rpgt.filingNote: Since 1 Jan 2025, RPGT is self-assessed: compute and e-file via MyTax within 60 days of disposal.
rpgt.disclaimer: Estimate only. Low/medium-cost homes below RM200,000 and certain transfers (e.g. between family members) may be exempt. Confirm with LHDN or a tax agent.

stampduty.inputSubtitle: Duty on the transfer (MOT) and the loan agreement when you buy property.
stampduty.priceHint: Stamp duty is on the higher of price or market value
stampduty.foreignerNote: Foreign buyers currently pay a flat 4%. Budget 2026 proposed raising this to up to 8% — not yet enacted; verify before relying on it.
stampduty.loanHint: 0 if cash purchase. Loan agreement duty is 0.5%.
stampduty.firstTimeBuyer: First-time homebuyer (residential ≤ RM500,000 — full exemption, extended to 31 Dec 2027)
stampduty.fullyExempt: Fully exempt as a first-time homebuyer.
stampduty.disclaimer: Estimate only. Other exemptions may apply (e.g. transfers between family members). Confirm with a lawyer or LHDN STAMPS.

blog.title: Tax Guides & Updates
blog.description: Practical guides on Malaysian personal tax, corporate tax, SST and e-Invoice — written in plain language and kept up to date.
blog.empty: No articles yet. Check back soon.
blog.ctaDesc: MYTax's free calculators turn these rules into your actual figures in seconds.
blog.disclaimer: This article is general information, not professional tax advice. Rules may change — verify with LHDN or a licensed tax agent before acting.

taxcomp.inputTitle: From Accounting Profit to Tax Payable
taxcomp.inputSubtitle: Enter figures from your P&L and the worksheet converts them into chargeable income, the way a tax agent prepares a tax computation.
taxcomp.pbtHint: From your profit & loss statement. Enter a negative number for a loss.
taxcomp.addBacksTitle: Add back: non-deductible expenses in P&L
taxcomp.addBacksHint: Expenses recorded in accounts that tax law does not allow
taxcomp.nonTaxableTitle: Less: income in P&L not taxable
taxcomp.nonTaxableHint: Income credited in accounts that is not business income for tax
taxcomp.caCurrentHint: Use the Capital Allowance tab to compute this
taxcomp.lossBfHint: Unutilised losses from prior years (max 10 YAs)
taxcomp.donationsHint: To LHDN-approved institutions; capped at 10% of aggregate income
taxcomp.smeQuestion: Does the company qualify for SME rates?
taxcomp.smeHint: Paid-up capital ≤ RM2.5M, revenue ≤ RM50M, not >20% foreign-owned, not a subsidiary of a large company. Check with the Corporate Tax page.
taxcomp.donationsLost: Donations above 10% cap (not deductible, not c/f)
taxcomp.disclaimer: Simplified single-business computation for estimation. Items like interest restriction, double deductions, group relief and investment incentives are not covered — engage a licensed tax agent for filing.

soleprop.inputSubtitle: Business owners are taxed at individual rates, not corporate rates. Enter your business figures to estimate your Form B tax.
soleprop.expensesHint: Rent, wages, supplies, utilities — wholly & exclusively for the business
soleprop.caHint: On equipment/vehicles — replaces depreciation (use the Capital Allowance tab)
soleprop.reliefsHint: Includes the automatic RM9,000 personal relief + EPF, lifestyle, insurance, etc.
soleprop.disclaimer: Simplified estimate. Partnership profit is divided among partners and taxed separately. Business losses can be carried forward. File using Form B by 30 June. Confirm with LHDN or a tax agent.

wht.inputSubtitle: When you pay a non-resident, you must withhold tax and remit it to LHDN. Pick the payment type to see the rate and form.
wht.desc_royalty: Royalty for use of rights, software, patents, etc. — 10%.
wht.desc_special_4a: Technical/advisory fees, services performed in Malaysia, rental of movable property, installation — 10%.
wht.desc_contract: Payment to a non-resident contractor for services — 10% (contractor) + 3% (their employees).
wht.desc_public_entertainer: Fees to a non-resident artist/performer for services in Malaysia — 15%.
wht.desc_other_4f: Other gains or profits under s4(f) — 10%.
wht.useDta: Apply a lower Double Taxation Agreement (DTA) treaty rate
wht.dtaApplied: Reduced treaty rate applied — keep the tax-residence certificate (COR) on file.
wht.remittanceNote: Remit to LHDN within 1 month of paying or crediting the non-resident, or a 10% penalty applies and the expense may be disallowed.
wht.disclaimer: Standard rates shown. A DTA may reduce or exempt the tax — verify the treaty and obtain a Certificate of Residence. Confirm with LHDN or a tax agent.

efiling.title: e-Filing guide: where each figure goes in Form e-BE
efiling.step1: Log in at mytax.hasil.gov.my and open e-Filing → e-BE (resident individual without business income).
efiling.step2: Copy each amount below into the matching field. Field names show in Malay/English on screen.
efiling.step3: Compare the final tax computed by e-Filing with ours — they should match closely (small rounding differences are normal).
efiling.fieldEmployment: Statutory income from employment — "Penggajian" (use total from your EA form)
efiling.fieldDividend: Dividends — "Dividen" (only taxable dividends above RM100k from YA2025)
efiling.fieldOtherIncome: Interest, discounts, royalties & other gains — "Faedah, Diskaun, Royalti"
efiling.fieldTotalIncome: Aggregate income — "Pendapatan Agregat" (computed by the form; use to cross-check)
efiling.fieldTotalReliefs: Total relief — "Jumlah Pelepasan" (enter each relief item one by one in Part D; this total is your cross-check)
efiling.fieldChargeable: Chargeable income — "Pendapatan Bercukai" (computed by the form)
efiling.fieldZakat: Zakat and fitrah — "Zakat dan Fitrah" (rebate section)
efiling.fieldTaxPayable: Total tax charged — "Jumlah Cukai Dikenakan" (computed by the form; cross-check)
efiling.fieldPcb: Monthly tax deductions (PCB/MTD) paid — "PCB yang telah dibayar" (check against your EA form)
efiling.fieldBalance: Balance of tax payable — "Baki Cukai Kena Dibayar"
efiling.fieldRefund: Tax paid in excess — "Cukai Terlebih Bayar" (LHDN refunds to your bank account)
efiling.reliefNote: Reliefs must be entered item by item in e-Filing (lifestyle, medical, EPF etc.) — keep receipts for 7 years. The total shown here is for cross-checking only.
efiling.disclaimer: Field labels follow the current e-BE layout and may shift slightly between years. This guide helps you transfer figures — the e-Filing form's own computation is final.

einvoice.title: e-Invoice Compliance Checker
einvoice.subtitle: Find out when LHDN MyInvois e-invoicing becomes mandatory for your business
einvoice.revenueBasis: Based on your audited financial statements for FY2022, or annual revenue as defined by LHDN for newer businesses.
einvoice.exemptDesc: Businesses with annual turnover of RM1 million or below are exempt from mandatory e-invoicing (threshold raised from RM500k, effective 1 January 2026). You may still adopt e-invoicing voluntarily.
einvoice.alreadyMandatory: e-Invoicing is already mandatory for your business since {date}.
einvoice.notYetMandatory: e-Invoicing becomes mandatory for your business on {date}.
einvoice.relaxationDesc: No penalties will be imposed until {date}, provided you show reasonable effort to comply (e.g. issuing consolidated e-invoices).
einvoice.rule10k: From 1 January 2026, every single transaction of RM10,000 or above requires an individual e-invoice — consolidated e-invoices are no longer accepted for these transactions.
einvoice.ruleExemption: Businesses with turnover ≤ RM1 million are permanently exempt (the formerly planned phase for smaller businesses was cancelled).
einvoice.ruleVoluntary: Exempt businesses may opt in voluntarily — buyers increasingly request e-invoices to claim tax deductions.
einvoice.ruleMyInvois: e-Invoices are submitted via the free MyInvois portal or through API integration with your accounting software.
einvoice.rulePhase4Relaxation: Phase 4 businesses (RM1M–5M) enjoy an extended relaxation period until 31 December 2027.
einvoice.disclaimer: Based on LHDN guidelines as updated June 2026. Requirements may change — verify with LHDN (hasil.gov.my) or your tax agent before making compliance decisions.

corptools.title: Corporate Tax Tools
corptools.subtitle: Tax computation, CP204, capital allowances, withholding tax and incentives — for Malaysian companies

cp204.estimatedTax: Estimated Tax Payable for the Year (RM)
cp204.priorYearHint: Optional — used to check the 85% minimum rule
cp204.belowMinimum: ✗ Below the 85% minimum rule — estimate must be at least {min}
cp204.installmentNote: {count} equal installments, starting from the 2nd month of the basis period, due by the 15th
cp204.noPenalty: Within the 30% buffer — no underestimation penalty.
cp204.disclaimer: New SMEs are exempt from CP204 for the first 2 years of assessment. Late installments incur a 10% penalty. For reference only — consult a licensed tax professional.

capalw.newVehicleQuestion: New vehicle costing RM150,000 or less?
capalw.smallValueNote: Small value assets (≤ RM2,000 each) get a 100% write-off. Total claim capped at RM20,000 per YA for non-SMEs; no cap for SMEs.
capalw.capWarning: Motor vehicle cap applied: cost {cost} but qualifying expenditure is capped at {qe}.
capalw.moreYears: ... and {count} more years (industrial buildings take up to 30 years)
capalw.disclaimer: Capital allowances can only be set off against income from the same business source. Unabsorbed allowances carry forward indefinitely. Accounting depreciation is not deductible. For reference only — consult a licensed tax professional.

incentives.title: Which incentives might apply to you?
incentives.noMatch: No common incentive matches this combination — consult MIDA or a tax advisor for niche schemes.
incentives.pioneer_benefit: 70% of statutory income exempt from tax for 5 years (100% for selected high-tech/strategic projects).
incentives.pioneer_eligibility: Companies undertaking promoted activities or producing promoted products (manufacturing and selected services). Cannot be combined with ITA for the same project.
incentives.ita_benefit: Allowance of 60% on qualifying capital expenditure incurred within 5 years, offset against up to 70% of statutory income (100%/100% for selected projects).
incentives.ita_eligibility: Alternative to Pioneer Status — better for capital-intensive projects with long gestation. Same promoted activity/product criteria.
incentives.ra_benefit: 60% allowance on qualifying capital expenditure for expansion, modernisation, automation or diversification, offset against up to 70% of statutory income. Available for 15 consecutive YAs.
incentives.ra_eligibility: Manufacturing companies (and selected agricultural projects) in operation for at least 36 months, reinvesting in the existing business.
incentives.ra_agency: LHDN (claimed in tax return — no prior approval, but keep project records)
incentives.gita_benefit: GITA: 100% allowance on qualifying green capex offset against 70% of statutory income. GITE: 70% income exemption for qualifying green service providers.
incentives.gita_eligibility: Green technology assets/projects (solar, energy efficiency, EV infrastructure, waste management) verified by MGTC; applications via MIDA/MGTC.
incentives.md_benefit: Reduced tax rate of 0%–10% on qualifying income for 5–10 years (new investment incentive structure), plus foreign knowledge-worker quotas.
incentives.md_eligibility: Companies undertaking qualifying digital activities (software, AI, cloud, fintech, etc.). Must meet MDEC's conditions including local spending and headcount.
incentives.automation_benefit: Accelerated capital allowance (100%) on qualifying automation equipment expenditure, within scheme caps.
incentives.automation_eligibility: Manufacturing (and selected services/agriculture) companies automating existing processes; machine must be verified (SIRIM/MIDA).
incentives.disclaimer: Incentive rates, periods and conditions change with each Budget and are project-specific. This is a starting point only — engage MIDA/MDEC or a licensed tax advisor before committing to an investment structure.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.collectDesc: MYTax does not store the personal financial data you enter into calculators. Calculator results are generated in your browser. When using the AI Tax Assistant, your messages are sent to our configured AI provider to generate a response and are not intentionally stored by MYTax after the request is completed.
privacy.useDesc: We may collect anonymous usage analytics (page views, feature usage) to improve the service. No personally identifiable information is collected through analytics.
privacy.storageDesc: Your tax calculation inputs are not saved to any server. If you use the share link feature, calculation parameters are encoded in the URL itself — no data is stored on our servers.
privacy.cookiesDesc: We use essential cookies only for language preference. No tracking or advertising cookies are used.
privacy.thirdPartyDesc: MYTax may use third-party analytics services that collect anonymous usage data. We do not sell or share any user data with third parties.
privacy.changesDesc: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated date.
privacy.contactDesc: For questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at support@mytaxs.online.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.acceptDesc: By accessing and using MYTax (mytaxs.online), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use the service.
terms.serviceDesc: MYTax provides free online tax calculation tools for Malaysian taxes, including personal income tax, corporate tax, PCB, employer contributions (EPF/SOCSO/EIS), and SST. The service also includes an AI-powered tax assistant for general tax queries.
terms.disclaimerDesc: All calculations and AI-generated responses are for reference and estimation purposes only. They do not constitute professional tax advice. Tax laws change frequently and individual circumstances vary. Always consult LHDN (Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia) or a licensed tax professional for official tax assessment and filing.
terms.liabilityDesc: MYTax shall not be held liable for any errors, omissions, or inaccuracies in the calculations or information provided. Users are responsible for verifying all results independently before making financial decisions.
terms.ipDesc: All content, design, and code on MYTax are the property of MYTax and are protected by applicable intellectual property laws. You may not reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works without prior written consent.
terms.changesDesc: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
terms.lawDesc: These Terms are governed by the laws of Malaysia. Any disputes arising from the use of this service shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of Malaysian courts.

footer.disclaimerNote: MYTax calculations and AI responses are for reference only and do not constitute professional tax advice. Always verify with LHDN or a licensed tax agent.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.s1Desc: MYTax provides tax calculators, content, and an AI assistant solely as informational tools to help you estimate your Malaysian tax position. None of the output constitutes professional tax advice, legal opinion, or accounting service.
disclaimer.s2Desc: We update tax rates, thresholds and reliefs based on publicly available LHDN guidance, but Malaysian tax law changes frequently. We do not guarantee that any calculation reflects the latest position. Always verify against LHDN's official portal at hasil.gov.my before filing or making financial decisions.
disclaimer.s3Desc: MYTax AI generates responses based on a knowledge base of LHDN public documents. AI models can produce errors, omissions or outdated information. You must not rely on AI answers as your sole basis for any tax decision. Treat AI output as a starting point for further research with LHDN or a licensed tax agent.
disclaimer.s4Desc: To the maximum extent permitted by Malaysian law, MYTax, its owners and operators shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential or punitive damages arising from your use of, or inability to use, the service — including but not limited to underpayment penalties, overpayment, missed deadlines or reliance on AI output. Your sole remedy is to discontinue use.
disclaimer.s5Desc: Using MYTax does not create a tax-agent, accountant, lawyer or fiduciary relationship between you and MYTax. For matters requiring a licensed professional opinion, please engage a qualified Malaysian tax agent or accountant.
disclaimer.s6Desc: The site contains links to LHDN, KWSP, PERKESO, RMCD and other third-party resources for your convenience. We do not control these sites and are not responsible for their content or accuracy.
disclaimer.s7Desc: We may update this Disclaimer at any time. Continued use after changes means you accept the updated version.
